title: Sanitizers, Static Analysis, and Fuzzing · Spiral Systems Review
summary: After a delay, a release reviewer reconstructs the layered memory-defect test matrix contract from partial traces, compares two near-miss implementations, and retrieves prior bounds and lifetime rules before feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for layered memory-defect test matrix after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Build and run representative tests with AddressSanitizer and LeakSanitizer, symbols, fatal findings, and supported-target notes.
Use UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer and fully instrumented MemorySanitizer environments for their distinct defect classes.
Run Clang and GCC static analyzers, preserve paths and assumptions, classify findings, and regression-test repairs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Tool Limit Matrix
steps.instruction:
review case — A review native-systems team handles memory case 8744. Produce bounded C evidence to classify a defect against complementary native tools; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Maintain a defect-by-tool and platform matrix so unsupported configurations and blind spots remain explicit.

Inspect the Tool Limit Matrix trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Tool Limit Matrix.
